summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer installs persistent crypto clipboard hijacker
details: Package name `@jaggle/resizeobserves` is a typosquat of the popular `@juggle/resize-observer` polyfill — the README even instructs users to `import { ResizeObserver } from '@juggle/resize-observer'`, but no ResizeObserver implementation ships in the tarball. Instead, the `postinstall` script (`npm-install.js`) `pip install`s a bundled Python package (`clipboard_guardian/guardian.py`) and registers persistent autostart under deceptive service names that impersonate OS-level Python runtime components: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` on Linux, a LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist` on macOS, and a Scheduled Task `PyRuntimeBroker` on Windows. On Linux it additionally invokes `sudo apt-get/pacman/dnf install -y xclip` non-interactively to satisfy its clipboard dependency. Once running, `guardian.py` polls the clipboard every 0.4s, regex-matches wallet addresses for 30+ cryptocurrencies (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Monero, Solana, etc.) and silently rewrites them to attacker-controlled addresses hardcoded in `DEFAULT_CONFIG` (e.g. ethereum `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`), seeded into `~/.config/clipboard-guardian/config.json`. Any crypto transfer the user copy-pastes after install is redirected to the attacker. The daemon also disguises itself via `setproctitle` / `SetConsoleTitleW` to match the impersonated OS service names and suspends activity when system monitors (Task Manager, htop, Activity Monitor, Process Hacker) are detected via psutil — explicit anti-forensics.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
